skill.name: happyhorse-1-0
skill.description: Use HappyHorse 1.0 on RunComfy to generate text-to-video clips with native 1080p output, synchronized audio, and multi-shot guidance.
skill.summary: Generate HappyHorse 1.0 text-to-video clips through the RunComfy CLI.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk hits, but most are false positives caused by markdown backticks, model version text, and shorthand URLs. Confirmed risks are intended external CLI execution, outbound RunComfy API calls, environment token use, token file storage, and generated output downloads. No prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, or hidden execution was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Intended External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to install and run the RunComfy CLI, including npx installation and runcomfy execution. This is core functionality, but it executes local commands and depends on the installed package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and workflow explicitly invoke runcomfy and npx. The usage appears legitimate for this skill, but local command execution is a real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Outbound API Calls With User Content And Token
medium_findings.description: The skill states that prompts and a bearer token are sent to the RunComfy Model API over HTTPS. This is expected behavior, but it exposes user prompts and credentials to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly names the RunComfy API endpoint and bearer token use. There is no evidence of unauthorized endpoints, but the data leaves the local machine.

medium_findings.title: Credential Storage And Generated File Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill documents token storage under the user config directory and downloads generated media to an output directory. These filesystem writes are legitimate, but users should understand where credentials and outputs are stored.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file locations and download behavior are documented in the skill. The text also describes owner-only token permissions, which reduces but does not remove the risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported many Ruby backtick execution findings, but SKILL.md is markdown documentation. The backticks wrap CLI names, endpoint names, schema fields, and prompt examples rather than executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanned file is markdown, and the flagged backticks are inline formatting or fenced command examples. No Ruby code or dynamic shell interpolation is present in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Path Traversal Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits correspond to version text, sample prose, and exit-code documentation. The path traversal hits are shorthand request URLs, not filesystem traversal or untrusted path construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic implementation and no filesystem path traversal logic. The flagged text is descriptive documentation only.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses runcomfy command examples to submit jobs and download generated outputs. Users should only run a trusted RunComfy CLI installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command pattern is explicit and intended. The risk is from local CLI execution, not from hidden malicious code in this markdown file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Submission Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill sends generation requests to model-api.runcomfy.net and downloads whitelisted RunComfy output URLs. This requires user consent to third-party processing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and output host rules are stated plainly. No unrelated network destination or callback behavior was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Token And Output File Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The CLI can read RUNCOMFY_TOKEN, store a token file, and write generated videos to a user-selected directory. This should be disclosed in marketplace warnings.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable, token file, and output download behavior are directly documented. The behavior is expected but security-relevant.

content.value_statement: Creating polished AI video prompts takes time and model-specific knowledge. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a ready workflow for HappyHorse 1.0 on RunComfy.
actual_capabilities: Documents the HappyHorse 1.0 text-to-video endpoint and supported input fields.
Provides RunComfy CLI commands for default, vertical, and lower-cost video runs.
Explains prompt structure for motion, camera direction, audio, and multi-shot consistency.
Lists duration, aspect ratio, resolution, seed, and watermark constraints.
Explains RunComfy exit codes, token handling, output downloads, and endpoint behavior.
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account, a valid token, and the RunComfy CLI.
Covers text-to-video only, not the separate image-to-video pipeline.
Does not run HappyHorse locally or avoid RunComfy costs and rate limits.
Video quality still depends on prompt clarity and provider availability.

use_cases.title: Produce short brand videos
use_cases.description: Create multi-shot product or campaign clips with consistent characters, wardrobe, and camera direction.

use_cases.title: Draft social video concepts
use_cases.description: Turn a brief into vertical video prompts with aspect ratio, duration, and audio direction already specified.

use_cases.title: Prototype cinematic sequences
use_cases.description: Generate fast test clips before committing to longer editing, stitching, or production work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic clip request
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user wants one simple video clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5 second HappyHorse 1.0 video about [subject]. Use 16:9, 1080p, clear motion, and natural ambient audio.

prompt_templates.title: Vertical social short
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 9:16 HappyHorse video prompt for [platform] showing [action]. Include camera framing, duration, lighting, and audio cues.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-shot character story
prompt_templates.scenario: Team needs consistency across two visual beats.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a two-shot HappyHorse prompt with the same [character description]. Shot one should show [beat]. Shot two should show [beat].

prompt_templates.title: Production-ready RunComfy plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wants execution details and constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a HappyHorse 1.0 RunComfy generation plan for [concept]. Include model fit, input settings, prompt, risks, and expected outputs.

output_examples.input: Generate a vertical cafe promo with warm audio.
output: A refined 9:16 prompt with espresso preparation, handheld close-up motion, warm morning light, and cafe ambience.
Recommended settings include 8 seconds, 1080p resolution, and watermark choice based on publication needs.

output_examples.input: Create a two-shot character scene in a rainy city.
output: A two-beat prompt that repeats the same character details in both shots for stronger visual consistency.
The response includes camera framing, rain sounds, interior ambience, and a reminder to keep the prompt under 2,500 characters.

output_examples.input: Explain whether HappyHorse is the right model for lip-sync.
output_examples.output: The skill explains that HappyHorse supports in-pass audio, but external audio-driven lip-sync should use another listed model.

best_practices: Describe motion over time instead of only describing a still image.
Repeat key character details in every shot when asking for multi-shot consistency.
Start with short 720p tests before spending on longer 1080p generations.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for unsupported aspect ratios or durations longer than 15 seconds.
Do not mix many unrelated actions into one short clip.
Do not send sensitive prompts unless third-party RunComfy processing is acceptable.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant create and run HappyHorse 1.0 text-to-video jobs through RunComfy.

faq.question: Does it require a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow needs the RunComfy CLI and either login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN value.

faq.question: Can it generate image-to-video outputs?
faq.answer: No. This skill covers the HappyHorse text-to-video endpoint only.

faq.question: Where are generated files saved?
faq.answer: The RunComfy CLI downloads generated outputs into the output directory chosen by the user.

faq.question: What video lengths are supported?
faq.answer: The documented duration range is 3 to 15 seconds.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning for CLI execution, network submission, token handling, and file downloads.
